text: If θ lies in the second quadrant, then θ is between 90 degrees and 180 degrees.
Part (i): For θ over 2, dividing the boundaries by 2 gives 45 degrees to 90 degrees.
Part (ii): For 2 θ, multiplying the boundaries by 2 gives 180 degrees to 360 degrees.
Part (iii): For negative θ, multiplying by minus 1 reverses the inequality, giving -180 to -90 degrees.
